# Transcrição: Here's_how_to_reframe_IMPOSTER_syndrome!

- **Duração:** 01:24

## [00:00 - 01:24]

How do you deal with imposter syndrome? Some people look at imposter syndrome as a voice in their head That's really mean a voice in their head that doubts them and it's almost like a sinister voice It's a voice that sounds like this. Oh, man. They're gonna work out that you're a dumb They're gonna work out that you're not who you say you are they're gonna work out that you're a fraud That's how imposter syndrome used to sound like in my head But I've reframed it and I've changed the voice of imposter syndrome in my head imposter syndrome now to me as a friend It's a friend that reminds me Vin Don't get complacent make sure that you live up to the expectations of others Make sure that you don't fall short Make sure that you keep your act sharp Make sure that you continually work hard to be able to teach and have impact and have good influence on those that you work with Don't get lazy Vin don't fall victim to mediocrity man, and that's how I've been looking at imposter syndrome It's no longer an enemy. It's no longer a foe. It's a friend because here's the alternative If you no longer have that voice in your head and you start thinking I'm the shit. I'm the best Keynote speaker. I'm the best magician. I'm the best communications skills teacher in the world I'll stop working on myself because if I believe I'm the best already What the hell is the point of improving because I'm already the best and that's why I look at imposter syndrome with a slightly different lens It's not something I'm trying to get rid of
